title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 17 - 2017: Worked on 20 Area Dimensions page

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his April 17, 2017 day in Boulder: he worked late into the night finishing his '20 Dimensions' website page and writing content, had a failed swim attempt due to not having nose clips, and reflects on quitting daily marijuana use after three years. He plans a long bike ride up toward Peak to Peak highway and mentions his triathlon training progress.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Finds comfort and enjoyment in it; sees it as a productive escape when feeling down
Choosing to stop after ~3 years of daily use; wants to taper off gracefully rather than abruptly
Prefers more frequent speed testing (weekly) than the recommended 6–8 week interval because he's improving quickly
Glad he skipped hard efforts the day before to allow recovery before pushing harder

And then this is what I look like when I'm out on the triathlon. I'm the guy in the 40 to 44 age group on Team Colorado Multisport. Yeah. So anyways. So what did I do yesterday? Yeah, I was kind of depressed yesterday about money or whatever.

I shouldn't talk about that. But yeah, so I was just like I went into my comfort zone, which is working on my website and I did some more. And then I felt water coming in. So I was like, I was like water was coming to my nose.

I went over to my main page and I added the thing about the intro to Livvy.
